key_points: The Sun is a star that provides light and heat to the Earth
The Earth is a planet that moves around the Sun
The Moon is the natural satellite of the Earth
The Earth rotates on its axis which causes day and night
The Moon reflects sunlight which makes it visible from Earth
examples: Day and night occur due to the rotation of the Earth
The Moon appears bright because it reflects light from the Sun
The Earth takes about 365 days to revolve around the Sun
The Moon takes about 27 days to revolve around the Earth
formulas: Rotation → Spinning of the Earth on its axis
Revolution → Movement of the Earth around the Sun
exam_tips: Remember the difference between rotation and revolution
Learn the relationship between the Earth, Moon and Sun
Understand how day and night occur
Practice simple diagrams of the solar system
